Paragraph

Suppose that \(f\) is a bounded real-valued function defined on the rectangle \(R\text{,}\) \(\cP\) a partition of \(R\text{,}\) and a point \(\bx_{\alpha}\in R_{\alpha}\) is chosen for each sub rectangle \(R_{\alpha}\) of \(\cP\text{.}\) We define the corresponding Riemann sum as
\begin{equation*} R(f, \cP, \{\bx_{\alpha}\}):=\sum_{\alpha} f(\bx_{\alpha}) \vert R_{\alpha} \vert. \end{equation*}
in-context
